title: Basilicata Watch: A Three-Day Climb at the Pump
dek: In Italy's least-populated mainland region, 267 registered filling stations tell a very local fuel story — and right now, it's pointing up.
body: Basilicata rarely makes fuel-price headlines, which is exactly why it's worth a closer look. Over the last three tracked days, the region's self-service Benzina average climbed from €1.967 to €1.984, and Gasolio moved from €2.141 to €2.175 — both running a few cents above the national self-service average of €1.970 and €2.167 respectively.

It's a small gap, but it's the kind of gap that only becomes visible when you can actually compare a region against the country in real time. Of Basilicata's 267 registered stations, 239 reported a price today — a 90% reporting rate that itself is a useful signal about how active the local network really is, not just what it charges.

None of this required a phone call to a regional office or a spreadsheet somebody had to remember to update. Fuel Dashboard rebuilds these figures — national, and for every Regione, Provincia, Comune and Gestore in Italy — automatically, every day, straight from the transparency feed. It's the same story we'd have needed a small analytics team and a lot of manual CSV-wrangling to tell a few years ago; now it's a page that refreshes itself.
pull_quote: Three straight days of increases in Basilicata — caught automatically, before any spreadsheet would have noticed.
caption:
Today's regional spotlight: Basilicata. Benzina €1.984/L, Gasolio €2.175/L — both a few cents above the national self-service average, and both climbing for a third straight day.

I built Fuel Dashboard (https://adeo-blip.github.io/fuel-price-italy/) to catch exactly this kind of local detail automatically: national trends, or drilled down to any Regione, Provincia, Comune or Gestore, with live registered-vs-reporting station counts. Basilicata alone has 267 registered stations, 239 of them reporting today.

What used to take a manual spreadsheet and someone remembering to refresh it is now a live, self-updating dashboard — built entirely with Claude Code, in English, Italiano, Français, Русский and 中文.

Take a look: https://adeo-blip.github.io/fuel-price-italy/

#FuelPrices #Italy #Basilicata #DataVisualization #ClaudeCode #Automation #Dashboard
